food_subgroup: Steamed or live whole fish — grouper (garoupa)

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic grouper food entry; no specific Long Hu hybrid grouper entry exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC covers grouper and seabass as a category. No specific Long Hu hybrid grouper code exists; closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept found for Long Hu hybrid grouper or generic grouper fish food item.

main_ingredients: Long Hu grouper (hybrid of giant grouper Epinephelus lanceolatus and tiger grouper Epinephelus fuscoguttatus)

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of Long Hu Garoupa is one whole fish of approximately 1 kg live weight, yielding roughly 700 g of edible flesh and sauce after steaming, shared among 4–6 diners at a Zi Char or seafood restaurant table.

nutrient_source.title: Grouper, raw — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 15033) and FAO/INFOODS SEA Food Composition Table for Southeast Asia, grouper (Epinephelus spp.) steamed values
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for grouper (raw) and cross-referenced with FAO/INFOODS SEA grouper steamed values. Long Hu is a hybrid grouper (Epinephelus lanceolatus × E. fuscoguttatus); no species-specific entry exists in any approved database. Steaming with soy sauce and sesame oil adds modest sodium and fat; values adjusted accordingly. Core macros are consistent across both sources for grouper species.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of protein-dominant fish and seafood foods — University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC (2008) International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values, Diabetes Care 31(12):2281–2283
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Long Hu Garoupa or hybrid grouper. GI is estimated at 5 based on the established principle that protein-dominant, near-zero-carbohydrate fish and seafood foods have negligible glycemic response. This is consistent with University of Sydney GI Database entries for steamed white fish and lean fish (GI range 0–10). Glycemic load per 700 g serving calculated as (0.5 g carbohydrate per 100 g × 700 g / 100) × GI / 100 = 0.035 × 5 = 0.175, rounded to 0.2.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content from soy sauce used in steaming preparation
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should be aware that the soy sauce and seasoning used in steamed Long Hu Garoupa contribute approximately 320 mg sodium per 100 g; a shared restaurant portion (150–200 g per person) may deliver 480–640 mg sodium, representing 20–28% of the 2,300 mg daily limit, and sauce consumption should be minimised.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very low carbohydrate content and high-quality lean protein
type2_diabetes.guidance: Long Hu Garoupa is an excellent protein choice for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes, as its carbohydrate content is negligible (0.5 g per 100 g), its glycemic index is effectively zero, and its high-quality lean protein (20.1 g per 100 g) supports satiety and glycemic stability without postprandial glucose excursion.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Lean fish with favourable omega-3 polyunsaturated fatty acid profile
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Long Hu Garoupa provides a lean protein source with a favourable polyunsaturated-to-saturated fat ratio (approximately 1.4:1) and meaningful omega-3 content, supporting cardiovascular health; however, individuals with cardiovascular disease should request reduced soy sauce to limit sodium intake from the steaming preparation.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Long Hu Garoupa (龙虎斑, Lóng Hǔ Bān) is a premium hybrid grouper fish produced by crossing the giant grouper (Epinephelus lanceolatus) with the tiger grouper (Epinephelus fuscoguttatus). The name 'Long Hu' (龙虎, Dragon Tiger) reflects the parentage of the two species. It is highly prized in Malaysian and Singaporean Zi Char and seafood restaurant cuisine for its firm, white, moist flesh, mild sweet flavour, and large yield of boneless meat relative to body size. The fish is typically sold live at market weight of 1 kg and prepared whole by steaming over high heat for 8–12 minutes, then finished with a pour of hot sesame oil and garnished with julienned ginger, spring onion, and light soy sauce — a classic Cantonese-style steamed fish preparation. The texture is tender yet firm, with a clean oceanic sweetness and no strong fishy odour. Nutritionally, Long Hu Garoupa is a high-protein, low-fat, low-carbohydrate food providing approximately 118 kcal, 20.1 g protein, and 3.5 g fat per 100 g. It is an excellent source of vitamin B12, selenium, phosphorus, and niacin. The glycemic index is negligible, making it highly suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or metabolic syndrome. The primary nutritional concern is sodium from soy sauce seasoning, which is relevant for individuals with hypertension or cardiovascular disease. At SGD 130 per kilogram at Marina Seafood House (Boat Quay), it is positioned as a premium restaurant dish. It is halal-certified in most Singapore and Malaysian seafood establishments.
common_user_queries: Is Long Hu Garoupa good for diabetics?
How is Long Hu Garoupa cooked?

confidence_reason: No species-specific nutrient entry exists for Long Hu hybrid grouper (Epinephelus lanceolatus × E. fuscoguttatus) in any approved primary database. Values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy grouper (raw) and FAO/INFOODS SEA steamed grouper data, which represent the closest defensible analogues. Sodium is adjusted upward to reflect soy sauce contribution in standard steamed preparation. GI is an analogous estimate based on the near-zero carbohydrate content of lean steamed fish. Confidence is medium rather than high due to the absence of an exact species-specific approved source entry.
notes: 1. Long Hu Garoupa is a hybrid grouper (Epinephelus lanceolatus × E. fuscoguttatus); no species-specific entry exists in USDA FoodData Central, HPB Foodo, IMR Malaysia MyFCD, or FAO/INFOODS SEA. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy grouper (NDB 15033) and FAO/INFOODS SEA grouper steamed values. 2. Sodium_mg (320 mg per 100 g) reflects the steamed preparation with soy sauce and seasoning typical of Zi Char restaurant service; raw grouper sodium is approximately 60 mg per 100 g. 3. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero for lean steamed fish per USDA SR Legacy; no industrial trans fats present. 4. dietary_fibre_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero as this is a pure animal protein food with no plant-derived fibre. 5. GI is set to 5 as an analogous estimate for a near-zero-carbohydrate steamed fish; no direct GI measurement exists for Long Hu Garoupa. 6. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, and all vitamins and minerals are estimated from USDA SR Legacy grouper values and are marked as adapted. 7. The input record listed cuisine_tags including 'Crab', which appears to be a field survey platform tagging artefact; Long Hu Garoupa is a fish dish, not a crab dish. The tag has been disregarded. 8. Price SGD 130 per 1 kg is preserved from the input as a contextual data point from field survey data (Marina Seafood House, Boat Quay) and is not used in nutritional calculations. 9. Sentinel values: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are all populated with estimated values from USDA SR Legacy grouper; no -1 sentinel values are used as reasonable estimates are available from the approved source.
